jobs.title: Business Analyst — IT Projects
jobs.description:
# Business Analyst — IT Projects

## About the Role

NexusPoint Systems is seeking a motivated Business Analyst to join our growing Operations team as we expand our cybersecurity capabilities and take on larger, more complex client projects. Reporting to Priya Sharma, Head of Service Delivery, you'll play a crucial role in bridging the gap between our technical teams and clients, ensuring project requirements are clearly understood and delivered successfully.

This graduate position offers an excellent opportunity to gain hands-on experience in IT project delivery within Western Australia's dynamic mid-market sector. You'll work directly with our cybersecurity and infrastructure teams while building strong relationships with clients ranging from professional services firms to mining operations.

As part of our 8-person Operations team, you'll be involved in some of our most significant projects, including major cybersecurity implementations and cloud migrations that are transforming how local businesses operate.

## Key Responsibilities

- **Requirements Gathering**: Conduct stakeholder interviews and workshops to capture detailed business and technical requirements for IT infrastructure and cybersecurity projects
- **Project Documentation**: Create clear, comprehensive project specifications, user stories, and process documentation that technical teams can implement effectively
- **Stakeholder Communication**: Serve as the primary liaison between clients and our technical teams, translating complex IT concepts into business language
- **Project Support**: Assist with project planning, timeline development, and progress tracking alongside our Solutions Architect team
- **Process Improvement**: Identify opportunities to streamline project delivery processes and enhance client satisfaction
- **Quality Assurance**: Review project deliverables to ensure they meet client requirements and NexusPoint's quality standards
- **Client Presentations**: Support senior team members in presenting project proposals and progress updates to client executives

## What You'll Bring

- **Education**: Bachelor's degree in Business, Information Systems, IT, or related field (recent graduates welcome)
- **Communication Skills**: Excellent written and verbal communication abilities with experience presenting to diverse audiences
- **Analytical Mindset**: Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail when analyzing business processes
- **Technical Interest**: Basic understanding of IT infrastructure, cloud services, or cybersecurity concepts (formal experience not required)
- **Project Awareness**: Familiarity with project management methodologies (Agile, Waterfall) through coursework or internships
- **Stakeholder Management**: Demonstrated ability to work collaboratively with different personality types and seniority levels
- **Local Market Knowledge**: Understanding of Western Australian business environment preferred
- **Tools**: Proficiency in Microsoft Office suite, with experience in project management or documentation tools a plus

## What We Offer

- **Competitive Graduate Salary**: $65,000 - $72,000 plus superannuation
- **Professional Development**: $3,000 annual learning budget for certifications, conferences, and training
- **Mentorship Program**: Direct coaching from Priya Sharma and exposure to senior leadership team
- **Career Progression**: Clear pathway to Senior Business Analyst role within 18-24 months based on performance
- **Flexible Work**: Hybrid arrangement with 3 days in our Northbridge office, 2 days remote
- **Health & Wellness**: Comprehensive health insurance and gym membership subsidy
- **Team Culture**: Regular team events, quarterly company retreats, and collaborative work environment
- **Industry Exposure**: Opportunity to work with prominent WA businesses across mining, professional services, and manufacturing sectors

## How to Apply

Send your resume and cover letter to careers@nexuspoint.com.au with "Business Analyst — Graduate" in the subject line. In your cover letter, please explain why you're interested in the intersection of business and technology, and what you hope to achieve in your first role post-graduation.

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, with interviews beginning immediately. We're looking to fill this position by early February to support our upcoming project pipeline.

*NexusPoint Systems is an equal opportunity employer committed to building a diverse and inclusive team.*

jobs.title: Cloud Infrastructure Intern
jobs.description:
# Cloud Infrastructure Intern

**NexusPoint Systems** | Solutions Architecture Team | Perth, WA

Are you passionate about cloud technology and eager to gain hands-on experience with enterprise infrastructure? Join our Solutions Architecture team and help transform how mid-market businesses leverage cloud technology across Western Australia.

## About the Role

We're looking for a motivated Cloud Infrastructure Intern to support our growing cloud migration practice. Reporting to Senior Solutions Architect Liam Foster, you'll work directly on client projects while learning industry-standard tools and practices. This 6-month internship offers genuine exposure to AWS and Azure environments, infrastructure automation, and client-facing project work.

Our Solutions Architecture team of 4 professionals manages complex cloud migrations for organisations with 50-500 employees. You'll be contributing to real projects from day one, not just observing from the sidelines.

## Key Responsibilities

- **Cloud Migration Support**: Assist with discovery and planning phases for client cloud migrations, including environment assessments and dependency mapping
- **Infrastructure Documentation**: Create and maintain technical documentation for client environments, network diagrams, and migration runbooks
- **Infrastructure as Code**: Learn and implement Terraform and CloudFormation templates under senior guidance
- **Environment Monitoring**: Help configure monitoring and alerting for newly migrated workloads using CloudWatch and Azure Monitor
- **Client Deliverables**: Contribute to migration reports, technical recommendations, and project status updates
- **Lab Environment Management**: Maintain our internal testing environments for proof-of-concept work
- **Research & Analysis**: Investigate emerging cloud services and prepare briefings for the team on new AWS/Azure features

## What You'll Bring

- Currently studying Computer Science, Information Technology, or related field (2nd year minimum)
- Basic understanding of networking concepts (TCP/IP, DNS, VPN)
- Familiarity with at least one cloud platform (AWS or Azure) through coursework or personal projects
- Experience with Windows and Linux command line interfaces
- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
- Excellent written communication - you'll be contributing to client documentation
- Genuine interest in infrastructure automation and cloud architecture
- Ability to work independently while knowing when to ask for help

**Preferred but not essential:**
- AWS Cloud Practitioner or Azure Fundamentals certification
- Basic scripting experience (PowerShell, Bash, or Python)
- Understanding of virtualisation concepts

## What We Offer

- **$25/hour** with flexible hours to accommodate study commitments
- Mentorship from experienced cloud architects including direct guidance from Liam Foster
- **Certification support** - we'll cover AWS/Azure certification costs and provide study time
- Real client project experience with established WA businesses
- Access to our comprehensive lab environment with multiple cloud subscriptions
- Potential pathway to graduate position based on performance
- Modern office environment in Perth CBD with hybrid work options
- Team lunches and professional development sessions

## Growth & Learning

This internship is designed to accelerate your cloud career. Previous interns have gone on to full-time roles at NexusPoint or secured cloud engineer positions with our enterprise clients. You'll gain exposure to:

- Multi-cloud architecture patterns
- Enterprise security requirements 
- Client stakeholder management
- Infrastructure automation best practices
- Disaster recovery planning

## How to Apply

Send your CV and a brief cover letter explaining your interest in cloud infrastructure to careers@nexuspoint.com.au with "Cloud Infrastructure Intern" in the subject line. Include any relevant coursework, personal projects, or certifications.

**Application deadline**: Rolling basis - early applications encouraged

Questions about the role? Feel free to reach out to Jordan Reeves, our People & Culture Coordinator, at jordan.reeves@nexuspoint.com.au.

---

*NexusPoint Systems is committed to creating an inclusive workplace where all team members can contribute their best work.*

jobs.title: Junior Security Analyst
jobs.description:
# Junior Security Analyst

**Department:** Cybersecurity  
**Reports to:** Marcus Webb, Cybersecurity Lead  
**Location:** Perth, Western Australia  
**Employment Type:** Full-time

## About the Role

We're looking for an enthusiastic Junior Security Analyst to join our growing cybersecurity team at NexusPoint Systems. As we expand our security services to meet increasing client demand across Western Australia's mid-market, this is an exciting opportunity to build your career in a dynamic, supportive environment.

You'll work directly with Marcus Webb, our Cybersecurity Lead, as part of a close-knit 3-person security team that's at the heart of our strategic pivot toward comprehensive cybersecurity services. This role offers hands-on experience across multiple client environments while contributing to some of the largest security projects in our company's history.

## Key Responsibilities

- Monitor and triage security alerts from our SIEM platform, escalating critical incidents according to established procedures
- Assist with vulnerability assessments and penetration testing for mid-market clients (50-500 employees)
- Support incident response activities, including evidence collection and preliminary analysis
- Help maintain security documentation and compliance frameworks for client environments
- Conduct security awareness training sessions for client staff
- Participate in after-hours on-call rotation (1 week per month) with appropriate compensation
- Collaborate with our Service Desk team led by Sam Okoro to resolve security-related tickets
- Assist with security architecture reviews alongside Senior Solutions Architect Liam Foster
- Support preparation of security reports and recommendations for client leadership teams

## What You'll Bring

**Essential:**
- Bachelor's degree in Cybersecurity, IT, Computer Science, or related field
- Understanding of core security concepts: firewalls, intrusion detection, vulnerability management
- Basic knowledge of networking protocols (TCP/IP, DNS, HTTP/HTTPS)
- Familiarity with Windows and Linux operating systems
- Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail
- Excellent written and verbal communication skills for client interaction
- Eligibility for Australian Government security clearance (preferred)

**Desirable:**
- Internship or work experience in IT or cybersecurity
- Industry certifications (Security+, Network+, or equivalent)
- Experience with SIEM tools (Splunk, QRadar, or similar)
- Scripting knowledge (PowerShell, Python, or Bash)
- Understanding of cloud security concepts (Azure, AWS)

## What We Offer

- **Competitive salary:** $65,000 - $75,000 + superannuation
- **Professional development:** $3,000 annual training budget for certifications and courses
- **Flexible arrangements:** Hybrid work options (2-3 days in office)
- **Career growth:** Clear pathway to Senior Analyst role within 18-24 months
- **Mentorship:** Direct guidance from Marcus Webb and exposure to senior team members
- **Modern tools:** Latest security technologies and cloud-based infrastructure
- **Health benefits:** Private health insurance subsidy after 6 months
- **Team culture:** Regular team lunches and quarterly social events
- **Location:** Modern office in Perth CBD with excellent transport links

As a growing company (35 employees, $6.2M revenue), you'll have genuine impact on our direction while working with some of WA's most established mid-market businesses including mining, healthcare, and professional services clients.

## How to Apply

Send your resume and a brief cover letter explaining your interest in cybersecurity to Jordan Reeves at careers@nexuspoint.com.au. Please include "Junior Security Analyst" in the subject line.

We're committed to building a diverse team and encourage applications from all qualified candidates regardless of background.

**Applications close:** 3 weeks from posting date  
**Start date:** Flexible, with preference for candidates available within 4-6 weeks

additional_postings.title: Junior Cybersecurity Analyst | Growing Perth MSP
additional_postings.description: Our valued client NexusPoint Systems is seeking a Junior Cybersecurity Analyst to join their expanding security practice. This is a fantastic opportunity for a recent graduate to work alongside experienced practitioners on real client engagements. The successful candidate will support SIEM monitoring, vulnerability assessments, and incident response activities.

jobs.title: Marketing & Communications Coordinator
jobs.description:
# Marketing & Communications Coordinator

## About the Role

NexusPoint Systems is seeking a talented Marketing & Communications Coordinator to join our growing team and help position us as Western Australia's premier security-first managed service provider. Reporting directly to Managing Director Alex Nguyen, this part-time role (24 hours per week) is perfect for a marketing professional looking to make a significant impact in a dynamic, tech-focused environment.

As we expand our cybersecurity capabilities and pursue larger enterprise contracts, we need someone who can translate our technical expertise into compelling content that resonates with mid-market business leaders. You'll be the voice behind our digital presence, working closely with our Cybersecurity Lead Marcus Webb and Head of Service Delivery Priya Sharma to showcase our security-first approach.

## Key Responsibilities

- **Content Creation**: Develop case studies, whitepapers, and blog posts that demonstrate our cybersecurity expertise and successful client outcomes
- **Social Media Management**: Manage our LinkedIn presence with regular posts highlighting industry insights, team achievements, and thought leadership
- **Email Marketing**: Create and distribute monthly newsletters to our client base and prospect database
- **Website Content**: Collaborate with our team to keep website content fresh, accurate, and aligned with our security-first positioning
- **Marketing Collateral**: Design and produce sales materials, service sheets, and presentation templates for our business development efforts
- **Event Support**: Coordinate our participation in local business events and industry conferences
- **Brand Management**: Ensure consistent messaging across all channels that positions NexusPoint as the trusted cybersecurity partner for WA businesses

## What You'll Bring

- **Experience**: 2-4 years in marketing communications, preferably in B2B tech or professional services
- **Content Skills**: Strong writing abilities with experience creating technical content for business audiences
- **Digital Marketing**: Proficiency with LinkedIn, email marketing platforms, and content management systems
- **Design Capabilities**: Basic graphic design skills using tools like Canva or Adobe Creative Suite
- **Industry Knowledge**: Understanding of cybersecurity concepts and managed IT services (or willingness to learn quickly)
- **Communication**: Excellent written and verbal communication skills with attention to detail
- **Initiative**: Self-starter who can work independently while collaborating effectively with technical teams
- **Local Knowledge**: Familiarity with the WA business landscape is highly valued

## What We Offer

- **Competitive Salary**: $25-30 per hour based on experience
- **Flexible Schedule**: 24 hours per week with flexible timing to suit your lifestyle
- **Growth Opportunity**: Join us during an exciting expansion phase with potential for increased hours and responsibilities
- **Learning Environment**: Work directly with cybersecurity experts and gain valuable industry knowledge
- **Modern Workspace**: Our Subiaco office features standing desks, quality coffee, and a collaborative atmosphere
- **Professional Development**: Budget for courses, certifications, and conference attendance
- **Team Culture**: Join a close-knit team of 35 professionals who value work-life balance and continuous improvement

## How to Apply

Ready to help us tell the NexusPoint story? Send your resume and a brief cover letter explaining why you're excited about this role to [careers@nexuspointsystems.com.au](mailto:careers@nexuspointsystems.com.au). Please include "Marketing Coordinator Application" in the subject line.

We'd love to see examples of your work, so feel free to include links to content you've created, social media accounts you've managed, or marketing campaigns you've been involved with.

Applications close Friday, 15th December 2024. We're looking to fill this position in early January, with an immediate start available for the right candidate.

*NexusPoint Systems is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating an inclusive workplace for all team members.*

jobs.title: Service Desk Analyst
jobs.description:
# Service Desk Analyst

**Department:** Service Delivery  
**Location:** Perth, Western Australia  
**Employment Type:** Full-time  
**Reports To:** Sam Okoro, Service Desk Team Lead

## About the Role

Join NexusPoint Systems as our newest Service Desk Analyst and become the first point of contact for our growing client base across Western Australia. As part of our 6-person Service Delivery team, you'll provide tier 1 technical support while gaining exposure to cloud technologies, cybersecurity tools, and enterprise IT infrastructure that serves organisations from 50-500 employees.

This role is perfect for an IT graduate who enjoys problem-solving and wants to build their career in a fast-growing managed services environment. You'll work directly with Sam Okoro, our experienced Service Desk Team Lead, in a collaborative team that values learning and professional development.

With NexusPoint expanding our cybersecurity capabilities and pursuing larger enterprise contracts, this is an exciting time to join our team and grow alongside the business.

## Key Responsibilities

- **First-Line Support:** Handle incoming support tickets via phone, email, and our ticketing system, providing timely resolution for common IT issues
- **Microsoft 365 Administration:** Manage user accounts, distribution lists, SharePoint permissions, and basic Exchange configurations
- **Hardware & Software Troubleshooting:** Diagnose and resolve desktop, laptop, printer, and mobile device issues for clients
- **Documentation:** Maintain accurate ticket records, update knowledge base articles, and document recurring issues and solutions
- **Client Communication:** Provide clear, professional communication to end-users, ensuring they understand resolution steps and preventive measures
- **Escalation Management:** Identify complex issues requiring tier 2 support and ensure smooth handover to senior technical staff
- **Monitoring Support:** Assist with basic system monitoring and alert triage using our RMM tools
- **Asset Management:** Help maintain client hardware and software inventories in our PSA system

## What You'll Bring

**Essential Requirements:**
- Degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related field (recent graduates encouraged to apply)
- Strong understanding of Windows 10/11, macOS, and Microsoft 365 suite
- Experience with Active Directory basics, including user account management
- Excellent verbal and written communication skills with ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical users
- Customer service mindset with patience and empathy when dealing with frustrated users
- Basic networking knowledge (TCP/IP, DNS, DHCP concepts)
- Ability to work independently while knowing when to escalate issues

**Desirable Skills:**
- ITIL Foundation certification or willingness to obtain
- Experience with ticketing systems (we use ConnectWise Manage)
- Familiarity with remote support tools
- Basic PowerShell scripting knowledge
- Understanding of cybersecurity fundamentals

## What We Offer

- **Competitive Salary:** $55,000 - $65,000 + superannuation based on experience
- **Professional Development:** Annual training budget, certification support, and mentorship from senior team members
- **Career Growth:** Clear pathway to tier 2 support roles and specialisation opportunities in cybersecurity or cloud architecture
- **Flexible Work:** Hybrid working arrangements after successful probation period
- **Team Environment:** Small, collaborative team where your contributions are recognised and valued
- **Modern Tools:** Access to latest technology and industry-standard tools for learning and development
- **Benefits:** Health insurance subsidy, annual team events, and casual dress code

## How to Apply

Ready to start your IT career with a growing managed services provider? Send your resume and a brief cover letter explaining why you're interested in joining the NexusPoint team to careers@nexuspoint.com.au.

Please include "Service Desk Analyst" in the subject line and tell us about a time you helped someone solve a technical problem.

We review applications as they arrive and will contact suitable candidates within one week. NexusPoint Systems is an equal opportunity employer committed to building a diverse and inclusive team.

---

*NexusPoint Systems - Connecting technology, empowering business*
